patterns_recognized: D-grade dual/triple-opposing-signal picks (SP xERA against + PQI against) split close to 50/50 directionally across the archive (7/9, 7/11 postgames) -- the correct response is always SKIP, never an OVERRIDE fade to the opposing team; applied to PIT@CLE, TEX@ATL, MIN@CHC, SD@KC, DET@LAA, SF@SEA today.

HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) has a documented false-negative risk on gaps below the 0.04 'meaningful' threshold -- the 7/10 KC@BAL break (+0.018 gap, still won 5-3) is the precedent. TB@BOS (+0.0117) and LAD@NYY (+0.0176) both fall well under that line today; LAD@NYY additionally clears the pre-cap score>=5 four-dimension rescue bar validated twice (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA) and was elevated back to GOLD.

Extreme edges in the 18-25pp+ range remain a recurring calibrator-hallucination signature rather than real value (7/9 PHI@CIN +28.36pp HARD CAP 6 case, model was directionally wrong) -- TEX@ATL (+18.81pp) and WSH@OAK (+19.00pp) both treated as noise, not upside, today.
HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD/DIAMOND prevention) remains the single most validated, non-negotiable cap in the archive -- fired cleanly on CHW@TOR (-6.64pp) today with no override consideration.

Large opposing PQI deltas (|delta|>15) have reliably tracked with directional losses across recent slates (SD@KC, CHC@CIN, NYY@WSH 7/11) -- treated as a real qualitative caution, not just a scoring artifact, in today's SKIP confirmations (SD@KC, STL@ARI).

TB @ BOS.reasoning: No sportsbook match (odds_status=no_match) -- fair_prob/edge_pp are both null, so this pick is structurally unbettable regardless of the pre-cap A- stack. HARD CAP 7 also fired on a marginal +0.0117 bullpen gap, well under the 0.04 threshold flagged in the 7/10 KC@BAL false-positive (+0.018 gap, BAL still won). Missing market data alone forces SKIP.

LAD @ NYY.reasoning: Pre-cap score=5 with all four dimensions affirming (F-signal, SP xERA +1.16 favoring Cole over Sasaki, PQI confirms +11.2, Stage 1/2 agree Δ=0.04) matches the twice-validated CAP-10 rescue profile (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA). HARD CAP 7 fired too, but on a +0.0176 gap -- below the 0.04 threshold that broke the 0-for-7 CAP-7 streak on 7/10 (KC@BAL, +0.018, won). Elevating to GOLD.

PIT @ CLE.reasoning: CLE's F5 read (75.7%) collapses to a near-coin-flip full-game number (51.3%), a 0.244 Stage 1/2 gap that the archive flags as unreliable when SP xERA also opposes (7/8 TOR@SF precedent). SP xERA and PQI both run against CLE -- classic home-favorite overconfidence (Heuristic #1). D-grade dual-opposing picks split near 50/50 in the archive, so no fade to PIT either.

TEX @ ATL.reasoning: TEX is picked against Chris Sale (SP xERA -2 against), with fair_prob at just 0.32 versus the model's 50.8% -- an 18.81pp edge sitting in the archive's calibration-caution 18-25pp band. PQI also runs against TEX. Dual-opposing D-grade signal -- SKIP per the established no-fade rule; the market's skepticism about a Sale start looks like real information, not noise.

CHW @ TOR.reasoning: Negative edge (-6.64pp) on a pre-cap A- (score 4) triggered HARD CAP 1, the single most validated cap in the archive (4-for-5 live; most recently correct on ATL@STL 7/10 at -11.61pp, and Sale/ATL twice). F1_xera_gap is also starred (thin Spencer Miles sample). Treating CAP 1 as authoritative math, not a re-litigation candidate.

MIA @ MIL.reasoning: Only F-signal and Stage-agreement affirm MIL; PQI runs against (Δ=-6.0) and both confidence_downgrade and acute_roster stress flags are active. Pre-cap score is just 1 -- far short of the score>=5 four-dimension bar used to rescue LAD@NYY. Can't verify the acute_roster detail live, so treating it as a caution rather than an override.

MIN @ CHC.reasoning: Clean D-grade dual-opposing stack -- SP xERA (Ober) and PQI both run hard against CHC (Δ=-15.0). Matches the repeated archive pattern (7/9, 7/11) where these configurations split close to 50/50 directionally; SKIP is correct and no fade to MIN is warranted given the genuine ambiguity.

BAL @ HOU.reasoning: Cleanest signal stack on the slate -- no hard cap fired. F-signal and PQI (+7.9) both confirm HOU, edge (13.49pp) sits comfortably inside the [4,15]pp band, Stage 1/2 agree (Δ=0.076, under the 0.12 trigger), and the bullpen gap is essentially flat (-0.0012) so CAP 7's pattern doesn't apply. HOU's 65.9% full-game probability is above the home-favorite-overconfidence watch line (Heuristic #1), but confirmed PQI+F-signal support offsets that caution.

SD @ KC.reasoning: Triple-opposing-signal D-grade: SP xERA, PQI (Δ=-22.9, one of the largest opposing deltas on the slate), and a negative team_quality modifier all run against KC. Same profile that correctly called several 7/11 D-grade losses. SKIP is correct; genuine directional ambiguity means no fade to SD.

CIN @ COL.reasoning: Thin pre-cap stack (score=1): only F-signal and Stage-agreement affirm, PQI runs against (Δ=+18.4) and confidence_downgrade is active. Edge (+3.1pp) sits below the 4pp floor. Falls well short of the four-dimension rescue bar applied to LAD@NYY.

DET @ LAA.reasoning: D-grade with SP xERA (Detmers) against LAA and a negative team_quality modifier; only Stage-agreement affirms. Thin, signal-opposed pick with no compensating F-signal or PQI support.

WSH @ OAK.reasoning: A 74.5% model probability against Oakland, with a 19.0pp edge sitting in the archive's calibration-caution band (18-25pp) -- the same signature behind the +28.36pp PHI@CIN hallucination on 7/9 (HARD CAP 6 territory, model was wrong). PQI also runs against WSH. Treating the extreme edge/probability combo as noise rather than value.

STL @ ARI.reasoning: PQI runs sharply against ARI (Δ=-25.8, one of the largest opposing deltas on the slate) with only Stage-agreement affirming. Edge (+16.24pp) is elevated but below the hard calibration-caution floor. Large opposing PQI deltas have reliably tracked with losses elsewhere in the archive (SD@KC, CHC@CIN).

SF @ SEA.reasoning: Clean D-grade dual-opposing stack with fair_prob (0.344) below the 0.42 floor -- the same calibration flag as TEX@ATL. SP xERA and PQI both run against SF. SKIP is correct with no fade to SEA given the genuine directional ambiguity documented in the archive.
